title: centrolepis drummondiana
intro: Centrolepis drummondiana, commonly known as Drummond's centrolepis or Drummond's spike-rush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Centrolepidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly in Australian indigenous culture, for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Centrolepis drummondiana include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and alleviate pain, making it a valuable remedy for treating minor cuts and scrapes. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a paste or infused in water to be used topically or taken orally, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions.
